description_long: This is a cylindrical flute with alternating bands of brown and olive green coloration, giving it a visually striking, segmented appearance. The flute features a series of evenly spaced finger holes along its length, with the largest hole near the bottom and smaller holes progressing upward. The mouthpiece end is slightly wider and capped, while the opposite end is open, typical of traditional woodwind instruments. The surface appears to be made of wood or a wood-like material, with a matte finish and subtle grain texture visible in the colored bands. There are no visible engravings, decorative inlays, or branding marks on the exterior. The overall design is simple and functional, suitable for use in street performances or casual urban music settings.
description: A cylindrical, banded brown and olive green flute with finger holes and a capped mouthpiece, made of wood with a matte finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the flute, with all finger holes and the capped mouthpiece clearly visible.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the alternating colored bands and the cylindrical shape, with the mouthpiece end prominent.
description_view_2: Top-down view focusing on the capped mouthpiece and the alignment of the finger holes along the body.
description_view_3: Rear angled view displaying the back side of the flute, with the colored bands and open end visible.
